meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve can't finish projects (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome can't finish projects. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Student Guide: Mastering User Onboarding to Finish Projects
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning projects. Learn how to implement advanced user onboarding using Antigravity and Claude Code to ensure your apps get real-world traction.
content.answer_block: To stop abandoning projects, shift your focus from feature-bloat to user onboarding. By prioritizing the 'Aha! moment' through guided flows, you validate your product's value early. Use Antigravity for rapid architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, turning half-finished codebases into polished, launch-ready applications that users actually understand.
content.intro:
The graveyard of abandoned GitHub repositories is paved with good intentions. You start with a vision, code for three weeks, and then hit the wall: the complexity grows, the excitement fades, and you move to the next 'shiny' framework. This isn't a failure of talent; it's a failure of focus. The secret to finishing isn't building more—it's building the right bridge between your code and the user.

User onboarding is the ultimate forcing function. When you force yourself to design a seamless introduction to your app, you stop adding unnecessary complexity and start solving actual user pain points. By utilizing modern AI-assisted engineering, you can build these experiences in hours rather than weeks, keeping your momentum high and your project scope laser-focused.

steps.title: Define the 'Aha!' Moment
steps.description: Identify the singular action a user must take to see value. Use AI to map the shortest path from sign-up to this outcome, eliminating all secondary features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project idea and define the critical path for a user to achieve the primary value proposition. Identify 3 unnecessary features that can be cut to simplify onboarding.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'map the user flow for onboarding and identify features to remove'

steps.title: Scaffold the Onboarding Context
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate for a guided tour or wizard that integrates with your existing state management.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular React onboarding wizard component that tracks user progress across 3 steps and integrates with a global AuthContext.

steps.title: Implement Intelligent State Persistence
steps.description: Ensure users can leave the onboarding and return exactly where they left off by persisting the progress to local storage or your DB.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a custom hook that persists onboarding progress to localStorage and syncs with a Supabase user profile table.

steps.title: Automate Success Metrics Tracking
steps.description: Instrument your onboarding flow to measure drop-off rates, ensuring you have data-driven insights to improve the process.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add PostHog analytics tracking to each step of my onboarding wizard to capture conversion events.

steps.title: Final Polish and Edge-Case Testing
steps.description: Use Claude Code to stress-test the onboarding flow for empty states and error handling.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the onboarding logic for potential race conditions or state synchronization issues when the user refreshes the page.

pro_tips: Never build a multi-step form when a single-input interaction can achieve the same result.
Use 'optimistic UI' updates during onboarding to make the app feel instant, even if the backend is processing heavy data.
Always include a 'Skip' button. Forcing users through onboarding creates resentment, not retention.
Implement 'feature flagging' for your onboarding flow so you can test two different versions without redeploying your entire codebase.
Treat your onboarding as a product in itself; if the onboarding is boring, the user assumes the app is boring.
content.next_steps: Deploy your project to Vercel or Netlify, share the link in our community discord, and gather feedback from at least 5 peers to iterate on your onboarding flow.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting half-finished ideas gather dust in your repository. By mastering the art of the onboarding flow, you ensure your work reaches the world and solves real problems. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
